2606.08312.title: Neuro-Symbolic Injection of LTLf Constraints in Autoregressive Reinforcement Learning Policies
2606.08312.paper_summary: In this work we study offline reinforcement learning (RL) under temporally extended task constraints expressed in Linear Temporal Logic over finite traces (LTLf). Recently, transformer-based approaches such as Trajectory Transformers and Decision Transformers have been adopted to address RL as a sequence modeling problem. However, these methods optimize purely for reward and do not account for high-level temporal requirements. Here, we introduce a neurosymbolic framework that injects LTLf background knowledge into such transformer-based RL policies. Our approach compiles LTLf formulas into deterministic finite automata (DFAs) and integrates them into the learning process through a differentiable representation and a logic-based loss function. In particular, we derive differentiable satisfaction signals from DFA progression and use them as a regularization term during training. The resulting method is architecture-agnostic across different models. We evaluate the proposed framework on navigation environments with specification suites covering combinations of safety and reachability temporal properties. Experimental results show that incorporating background knowledge not only improves constraint satisfaction, but also maintains competitive return compared to vanilla baselines.
2606.08312.paper_summary_zh: 在這項工作中，我們研究了在以線性時間邏輯（LTLf）表達的時間延伸任務約束下的離線強化學習（RL）。最近，基於Transformer的方法，如軌跡Transformer和決策Transformer，已被採用來將RL視為序列建模問題。然而，這些方法僅僅優化獎勵，並未考慮高層次的時間要求。在這裡，我們引入了一個神經符號框架，將LTLf背景知識注入這些基於Transformer的RL策略中。我們的方法將LTLf公式編譯成確定性有限自動機（DFA），並通過可微分表示和基於邏輯的損失函數將其整合到學習過程中。特別地，我們從DFA進展中推導出可微分的滿足信號，並在訓練過程中將其用作正則化項。所得到的方法在不同模型之間是架構無關的。我們在導航環境中評估了所提出的框架，這些環境的規範套件涵蓋了安全性和可達性時間屬性的組合。實驗結果顯示，納入背景知識不僅改善了約束滿足，還保持了與原始基準相比的競爭性回報。
2606.08312.author: Ashkan Ansarifard et.al.
2606.08312.authors: Ashkan Ansarifard, Matteo Mancanelli, Elena Umili, Fabio Patrizi
